threads.label: Iran / Hormuz
threads.status_line: Settebello tanker disabled in Gulf of Oman (8th vessel since April blockade); 3 Indian crew missing; Brent +3.44% to $94.60; Trump threatened more strikes; June 22 talks still on calendar but conditions deteriorating
expand_if: formal MOU signed
talks completely collapse (Iran withdraws entirely)
oil spike above $115
naval incident closes Strait
new major US or Israeli strike breaking the pullback
Lebanon ceasefire agreed (would remove Iran's stated precondition)
Trump public ultimatum to Netanyahu on Lebanon
India escalates response to missing/killed crew

threads.label: Israel / Lebanon
threads.status_line: No new escalation since Jun 7-8 exchange; Hezbollah still rejecting pilot zones; Lebanon ceasefire remains Iran's precondition for US deal; next talks Jun 22 week
expand_if: Hezbollah formally accepts or rejects pilot zones framework
major Israeli ground incursion
Hezbollah rocket barrage on Israeli cities
comprehensive ceasefire agreement with withdrawal timelines
UNIFIL withdrawal
US forces Israel to pause Lebanon operations
mass civilian casualty event
Trump public demand for Netanyahu to halt Lebanon ops

threads.label: Ukraine Peace Deadline
threads.status_line: Zelensky unconditional ceasefire proposal rejected by Putin; US bandwidth absorbed by Iran conflict; Polymarket: deal before Jun 30 very unlikely, before 2027 trader consensus
expand_if: Trump formally withdraws from mediation
new public deadline set
ceasefire agreed
major US pressure on Ukraine (aid suspension)
Russia agrees to any settlement framework
Zelensky concedes territorial compromise publicly

threads.label: UK Economic Stress
threads.status_line: FTSE 10,254 (+0.27%); Brent $94.60 (+3.44%); 30Y gilt 5.60%; 10Y 4.94%; US CPI 4.2% adds regional inflation pressure; BoE first hike priced September; Q1 GDP +0.6%
expand_if: gilt yield spike above 5.8% with upward trajectory
energy price cap revision announced
recession officially confirmed
Bank of England emergency action
government fiscal statement
Brent above $115 sustained

threads.label: UK Labour Leadership
threads.status_line: 95+ Labour MPs calling for Starmer to resign; Burnham on Makerfield ballot Jun 18 (Survation: Burnham 49%, Reform 39%); Streeting resigned cabinet; Starmer: will not walk away

Formal leadership challenge triggered
Starmer resigns

threads.label: Mearsheimer
threads.status_line: Netanyahu-blocking thesis confirmed by Iran's Lebanon precondition (Araghchi, Jun 8); Settebello adds diplomatic-military contradiction layer Mearsheimer identified; no new post or appearance

Trump-Netanyahu public confrontation matches Mearsheimer's framing
mainstream US policy community engages with the Netanyahu-veto argument

Backbench parliamentary bill to repeal exemption tabled
Further attacks on Sikh individuals
Sikh community organisations make formal complaint to police or EHRC

Colombian government or candidate statements on Southern Spear before Jun 21 runoff

threads.label: Ebola Bundibugyo — DRC / Uganda
threads.status_line: 598 confirmed DRC / 115 deaths as of Jun 10 (up from 452/88 on Jun 6); ~37 new cases/day; US State Dept adds $20M for border-state preparedness; PHEIC in force; no spread beyond DRC/Uganda

WHO raises alert further
vaccine supply or pipeline announcement

threads.label: Russia / NATO / Romania
threads.status_line: Constanta port drone Jun 5 confirmed Ukrainian origin via electronic jamming; NATO foreign ministers discussing drone-over-allied-territory protocols; no further incident since Jun 5

NATO Article 4 consultation formally invoked
Romania retaliates or requests reinforcement
NATO adopts shoot-down authority for drones over Allied territory

threads.label: Colombia Election 2026
threads.status_line: Runoff Jun 21: de la Espriella ~44% vs Cepeda ~41% (first round); de la Espriella favoured after Paloma Valencia endorsement; Southern Spear raised in US Senate hearing; 11 days to vote
expand_if: major polling shift before June 21 runoff
de la Espriella wins runoff
Cepeda wins runoff
significant violence or electoral irregularity
Petro makes major statement endorsing Cepeda
US response to Colombia criticism of Operation Southern Spear

threads.label: Syria Parliament

constitutional amendment proposed
international reaction to composition
Islamist-secular conflict in chamber

threads.status_line: Tournament opens Jun 11 (Mexico vs South Africa); England final friendly Jun 10 vs Costa Rica; England's first match Jun 17 vs Croatia in Dallas; 48-team event runs to Jul 19 final at MetLife Stadium; Fleetwood Mac farewell tour and tournament build-up already visible in charts

England eliminated

threads.label: Taiwan Strait Tension
threads.status_line: Taiwan fired HIMARS into Taiwan Strait for first time Jun 10 (live-fire drill in Taichung City, directed toward China); Trump paused follow-on HIMARS arms deal after Xi summit; China response not yet issued; minimal Western media coverage
expand_if: China issues formal military response or condemnation
Trump resumes or formally cancels Taiwan arms deal
PLA conducts counter-drill or naval exercise near Taiwan
US diplomatic statement on Taiwan independence or One China policy
Taiwan announces further HIMARS deployment or expanded capability
Incident in Taiwan Strait (vessel, aircraft)

retired_threads.label: Turkey — CHP / Opposition
retired_threads.retired_because: 8 consecutive stable days since last_changed 2026-06-01; Imamoglu corruption trial (from Mar 9) and espionage trial (from May 11) both ongoing but no verdict, release, or major new development this week
retired_threads.revive_if: Imamoglu released or verdict delivered; CHP headquarters sealed permanently; street protests escalate to general strike; EU or US formal condemnation; constitutional court ruling on Imamoglu detention

retired_threads.label: Sudan Famine
retired_threads.retired_because: 8 consecutive stable days since last_changed 2026-06-01; lean season started June as projected (already captured in last status); no new IPC famine declaration, funding announcement, or ceasefire
retired_threads.revive_if: IPC declares additional famine zones beyond the 14 at risk; major donor announces significant new funding; Western government makes Sudan a diplomatic priority; ceasefire between SAF and RSF; humanitarian corridor agreement; death toll receives prominent international media coverage

source_discoveries.name: Richard Haass — 'Home & Away' Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former CFR president arguing Trump is recreating the JCPOA he destroyed, and that regime change pressure produced a more hardline Iranian government — structural counter-frame to deal optimism

source_discoveries.name: L'Orient Today
source_discoveries.why_interesting: English-language edition of L'Orient Le Jour; takes a meaningfully different political line from Al-Nahar on Lebanese politics; useful for south Lebanon coverage

source_discoveries.name: Asharq Al-Awsat (English)
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Gulf-backed pan-Arab daily; broke the Syria parliament June 8 story before other outlets; consistently ahead on Gulf/Arab political transitions and has senior source access in Beirut and Damascus

source_discoveries.name: Velina Tchakarova — Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Vienna-based geopolitical analyst; Austrian/Central European perspective on Ukraine distinct from Washington/London axis; published 'The Ukraine War: The June Opening and Its Limits' Jun 4 — structural frame less invested in Ukrainian optimism or Western hand-wringing

source_discoveries.name: Alastair Crooke — Conflicts Forum Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former senior British diplomat and MI6 officer; argues Hezbollah advises Iran rather than takes orders, inverting the proxy narrative; Jun 2026 pieces frame Iran's posture as calculated 'escalatory deterrence' not desperate deal-seeking — structurally different from any Western mainstream analysis
